what_it_is: A UI component used to label, comment on, or visually explain parts of a network.
mental_model: Sticky notes and callouts placed on the network.
example: Notes explaining how the train system is wired together.

Annotate.meaning: Controls how annotations attach to operators and where they appear.

items.Operator Viewer: Links the annotation to an operator viewer.
items.Enable Interaction: Allows clicking and interacting with the annotation.
items.Close Operators Utility: Closes linked operators from the annotation.
items.Include in Order: Includes this annotation in ordering logic.

Layer Zone.Below Grid: Annotation appears under the network grid.
Layer Zone.Between Grid and Nodes: Annotation appears between grid and nodes.
Layer Zone.Above Nodes: Annotation appears on top of nodes.

items.Depth Layer: Controls depth stacking relative to other UI elements.

Text.meaning: Controls the text content and layout of the annotation.

items.Limit Body Text Width: Restricts how wide the text can be.

Settings.meaning: Controls annotation behavior and appearance mode.

items.Mode: What kind of annotation this is.
items.Network Box: Annotation behaves like a network box.
items.Body Word Wrap: Wraps body text to multiple lines.

OP Viewer.meaning: Controls how an operator’s output is shown inside the annotation.

items.OP Viewer Interactive: Allows interaction with the embedded viewer.

items.Operator Viewer: Links the annotation to an operator viewer.
items.Enable Interaction: Allows clicking and interacting with the annotation.

what_it_is: Controls an application window on your screen.
mental_model: The frame and rules of a computer window.
example: The window where you watch the train scene run.

Window.meaning: Controls how the TouchDesigner window appears and behaves.

items.Ignore Taskbar: Lets the window overlap the system taskbar.
items.Display: Which screen or monitor the window appears on.
items.Offset: Moves the window from its aligned position.
items.Shift to Single Display: Forces the window onto one display.
items.DPI Scaling: Scales the window for high-DPI screens.
items.Update Settings from Window: Reads size and position from the current window.
items.Borders: Shows or hides the window frame.
items.Include Borders in Size: Counts borders in width and height.
items.Cursor Visible: Shows or hides the mouse cursor.
items.Constrain Cursor: Keeps the cursor inside the window.
items.Allow Viewer Interaction: Allows clicking and interacting inside the window.
items.Allow Minimize: Allows the window to be minimized.
items.Window Pixel Format: Pixel format used by the window.
items.V-Sync Mode: Controls synchronization with the display refresh.
items.Draw Window: Turns window drawing on or off.

what_it_is: An operator that limits or connects how things can move.
mental_model: A physical rule or joint between parts.
example: Coupling train cars so they can move but not separate.

Constraint.meaning: Defines what kind of connection or limit exists.

items.Active: Turns the constraint on or off.
items.Actor Comp: Which physics system this belongs to.

Limits.meaning: Controls how far movement or rotation is allowed.

what_it_is: An operator that turns geometry into a physical object that can move, collide, and be affected by forces.
mental_model: A physical object in the world with weight and motion.
example: A train car that can roll, bump into things, and be pushed.

items.Initialize Actor: Resets the actor to its starting state.
items.Update Collision Shape: Rebuilds the collision shape from geometry.
items.Active: Turns physics on or off for this actor.

Kinematic State.Static: Does not move unless forced by code.

Collision Shape.Oriented Bounding Box: A rotated box around the object.
Collision Shape.Axis Aligned Bounding Box: A box aligned to world axes.

items.Infinite Mass: Object cannot be moved by forces.

items.Forces: External forces applied to the actor.
items.Continuous Collision Detection: Prevents fast objects from passing through others.

Flex.meaning: Settings for particle or fluid-style physics.

items.Enable Emission: Turns particle emission on or off.

what_it_is: The system that runs physics for actors.
mental_model: The physics engine that moves and updates all physical objects.
example: The rules engine that decides how all the train cars move, collide, and react.

Solver.meaning: Controls how the physics simulation runs.

items.Actors: Which actors are controlled by this solver.
items.Always Simulate: Keeps simulation running even if not visible.

what_it_is: An operator that pushes, pulls, or spins physical objects.
mental_model: An invisible push or twist applied to things.
example: Wind pushing a train car or a shove that starts it moving.

Force.meaning: Controls continuous force applied over time.

items.Active: Turns the force on or off.
items.Force: The direction and strength of the push.
items.Force Offset: Where the force is applied from.
items.Torque: A twisting force that causes rotation.
items.Impulse Force: A one-time push instead of a constant push.

Force Field.meaning: Controls how force spreads through space.

items.Active: Turns the force field on or off.
